title: White Bean Kale Soup
cScaleSummary: C1 because the only cholesterol contributor is the chicken stock; the beans, olive oil, vegetables, kale, herbs, lemon, and bread are plant-based and contribute 0 mg cholesterol, so the per-serving cholesterol remains modest.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the recipe is built around fiber-forward beans, kale, and vegetables, stays low in saturated fat, and develops richness through aromatics, tomato paste, simmering, and mashed beans rather than animal fat.
intro: This rustic white bean and kale soup turns a short list of pantry ingredients into something deeply comforting and satisfying. White beans give the broth body and richness without cream, while kale brings texture and substance. Onion, carrot, celery, garlic, rosemary, and a little tomato paste build the base, and some of the beans are mashed into the soup so it eats like a real meal. A squeeze of lemon at the end keeps the whole pot bright and balanced.

items.item: dried cannellini or Great Northern beans
extra virgin olive oil for finishing

stepGroups.title: Cook the Beans
items: Add the soaked beans, water, and bay leaf to a pot. Bring to a boil, then lower to a gentle simmer. Cook until the beans are tender, about 60 to 90 minutes. Reserve the bean cooking liquid and discard the bay leaf.

stepGroups.title: Build the Soup Base
items: In a soup pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion, carrot, and celery. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes until softened and lightly sweet. Add the garlic and cook 1 minute more. Stir in the tomato paste, rosemary, crushed red pepper, salt, and black pepper. Cook 1 to 2 minutes, stirring, until the tomato paste darkens slightly and the rosemary becomes fragrant.

stepGroups.title: Build the Body
items: Add about 2 cups of the cooked beans to the pot along with the chicken stock and 1 cup of bean cooking liquid. Bring to a simmer. Mash 1 cup of the beans into the broth with a potato masher or large spoon until the soup takes on a lightly creamy body.

stepGroups.title: Finish the Soup
items: Add the remaining beans and the kale. Simmer 15 to 20 minutes until the kale is tender and the broth is rich and unified. Add a little more bean liquid or stock as needed so the soup stays brothy but substantial.

stepGroups.title: Brighten and Serve
items: Stir in the lemon juice and parsley. Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Let the soup sit 10 minutes before serving. Serve with toasted whole grain bread and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.
